% ref: claim_3_24 (proof, with statement restated for readability)
% title: SigmaSeparationEquivalences
\def\rowref{claim\_3\_24}
\phantomsection\label{claim_3_24}

% --- statement (restated, synced verbatim from
% --- claim_3_24_statement_SigmaSeparationEquivalences.tex) -----------------
\begin{Rem}[SigmaSeparationEquivalences]
    Let $G = (J, V, E, L)$ be a CDMG (def \ref{def-cdmg}); throughout we use the notation of def \ref{not-cdmg}, the walk and path definitions of def \ref{def:walks}, items~i.\ (a \emph{walk} in $G$ is a finite alternating sequence $\pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n)$ for some integer $n \ge 0$, with $v_0, \dots, v_n \in J \cup V$ and $a_0, \dots, a_{n-1} \in E \cup L$, satisfying the walk constraint at every $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$:
    \[
        \bigl(a_k = (v_k, v_{k+1}) \;\land\; a_k \in E \cup L\bigr)
        \;\;\lor\;\;
        \bigl(a_k = (v_{k+1}, v_k) \;\land\; a_k \in E\bigr),
    \]
    with ``the same node may appear multiple times in a walk'') and~v.\ (a \emph{path} in $G$ is a walk in which no node occurs more than once: $\forall\, k, l \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\},\; k \ne l \implies v_k \ne v_l$), the collider classification of def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, item~ii.\ (``$k$ is a collider on $\pi$'' iff $\mathrm{ah}_\pi(k) = 2$), the $C$-$\sigma$-open/$C$-$\sigma$-blocked predicates of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.\ (``$\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open'' iff every collider position $v_k$ on $\pi$ lies in $\Anc^G(C) := \bigcup_{c \in C} \Anc^G(c)$ from def \ref{def_3_5}, item~iv., and every blockable non-collider position $v_k$ on $\pi$ lies outside $C$) together with its De Morgan dual (``$\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked'' iff $\pi$ is not $C$-$\sigma$-open --- the two notions are by construction mutually exclusive and jointly exhaustive over walks in $G$, per the De Morgan paragraph of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}), and the $i\sigma$-separation predicate ``$A \isPerp_G B \given C$'' (resp.\ its negation ``$A \nisPerp_G B \given C$'') of def \ref{def:sigma_separation}, items~1--2. Let
    \[
        A, B, C \ins J \cup V
    \]
    be subsets of nodes; $A$, $B$, $C$ are \emph{not} assumed to be pairwise disjoint, nor disjoint from the input-node set $J$, nor non-empty (matching the permissiveness of def \ref{def:sigma_separation}, item~1).

    \emph{Length of a walk/path (used by item~2 below).}
    For a walk $\pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n)$ in $G$ in the sense of def \ref{def:walks}, item~i., the \emph{length} of $\pi$ is the integer $n \ge 0$ recording the number of edge positions in the tuple (equivalently, the number of walk-steps $a_0, a_1, \dots, a_{n-1}$). The same definition is used for paths (which are walks per def \ref{def:walks}, item~v.). The minimum-length witnesses asserted in item~2 below refer to this length; the trivial walk $\pi = (v_0)$ at $n = 0$ has length $0$.

    Then by Proposition \ref{prp:sigma_opens} (= claim\_3\_23, the three-way equivalence between existence of a $C$-$\sigma$-open path, existence of a $C$-$\sigma$-open walk, and existence of a $C$-$\sigma$-open walk whose colliders all lie in $C$), we have the following two structurally distinct statements (statement~1 is an equivalence \emph{of} the $i\sigma$-separation predicate; statement~2 is a pair of existential consequences of its negation):

    \begin{enumerate}
        \item \emph{Equivalent characterisations of $A \isPerp_G B \given C$.}
            The predicate $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ of def \ref{def:sigma_separation}, item~1, is equivalent to each of the following two universal statements. (Throughout, the doubled writing ``$C$-$\sigma$-blocked by $C$'' of the literal LN block refers to the same set $C$ on both occurrences: the prefix ``$C$-'' fixes the conditioning-set parameter of the predicate ``$\sigma$-blocked'' of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, and the trailing ``by $C$'' merely re-states that conditioning set; no second set is in play.)
            \begin{enumerate}
                \item For every integer $n \ge 0$ and every walk
                    \[
                        \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n)
                    \]
                    in $G$ in the sense of def \ref{def:walks}, item~i., with $v_0 \in A$ and $v_n \in J \cup B$, the walk $\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked in the sense of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking} (i.e.\ \emph{not} $C$-$\sigma$-open per def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.). This is exactly the unfolded form of def \ref{def:sigma_separation}, item~1.

                \item For every integer $n \ge 0$ and every walk
                    \[
                        \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n)
                    \]
                    in $G$ in the sense of def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.\ which is additionally a \emph{path} in $G$ in the sense of def \ref{def:walks}, item~v.\ (i.e.\ $\forall\, k, l \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\},\; k \ne l \implies v_k \ne v_l$), with $v_0 \in A$ and $v_n \in J \cup B$, the walk $\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked in the sense of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking} (i.e.\ not $C$-$\sigma$-open per def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.).
            \end{enumerate}

            \emph{Length-zero/overlap corner case (side remark).} The walk quantifier in (1a) ranges over all admissible lengths $n \ge 0$, including the trivial walk $\pi = (v_0)$ at $n = 0$ admitted by def \ref{def:walks}, item~i. A trivial walk has no walk-step indices in $\{0, 1, \dots, n - 1\}$ and no position $k$ that is a collider or a blockable non-collider on $\pi$ in the sense of def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, item~ii.\ or def \ref{def:unblockable_noncollider}, so it is vacuously $C$-$\sigma$-open per def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1., hence \emph{not} $C$-$\sigma$-blocked. Whenever $A \cap (J \cup B) \ne \emptyset$, picking any $v \in A \cap (J \cup B)$, the trivial walk $\pi = (v)$ is a walk from $A$ to $J \cup B$ that fails the blocking condition; consequently, under (1a) (and equivalently (1b), since a trivial walk is also a path), the predicate $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ forces in particular $A \cap (J \cup B) = \emptyset$. This matches the convention already recorded in def \ref{def:sigma_separation}, item~1 (``Range of the walk quantifier'' paragraph), and is the canonical handling of the overlap corner case.

        \item \emph{Existential consequences of $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$.}
            Suppose $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$ holds in the sense of def \ref{def:sigma_separation}, item~2 --- equivalently, the universal condition (1a) of item~1 above \emph{fails}, i.e.\ there exists at least one walk from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ which is $C$-$\sigma$-open. Then both of the following existential statements hold (independently of each other; each follows from a separate clause of Proposition \ref{prp:sigma_opens}):
            \begin{enumerate}
                \item There exist an integer $n \ge 0$ and a walk
                    \[
                        \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n)
                    \]
                    in $G$ in the sense of def \ref{def:walks}, item~i., such that all four of the following conjuncts hold simultaneously:
                    \begin{itemize}
                        \item $v_0 \in A$;
                        \item $v_n \in J \cup B$;
                        \item $\pi$ is a path in $G$ in the sense of def \ref{def:walks}, item~v.\ ($\forall\, k, l \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\},\; k \ne l \implies v_k \ne v_l$), and $\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open in $G$ in the sense of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.;
                        \item the length $n$ of $\pi$ is \emph{minimum} among all non-negative integers $m \ge 0$ for which there exists a walk $\pi' = (v'_0, a'_0, v'_1, \dots, v'_{m-1}, a'_{m-1}, v'_m)$ of length $m$ in $G$ (def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.) with $v'_0 \in A$, $v'_m \in J \cup B$, $\pi'$ a path in $G$ (def \ref{def:walks}, item~v.), and $\pi'$ $C$-$\sigma$-open in $G$ (def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.) --- that is, the minimization range is exactly the class
                            \[
                                \mathcal{P}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}} := \bigl\{\, \pi' \text{ walk in } G \,\bigm|\, \text{start-node of } \pi' \in A,\ \text{end-node of } \pi' \in J \cup B,\ \pi' \text{ is a path},\ \pi' \text{ is } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open} \,\bigr\}.
                            \]
                            Per the addition tag \texttt{[shortest\_qualifier\_reference\_class\_ambiguous]}, the parenthetical ``(shortest)'' in the literal LN block is part of the existence claim: the witnessing path is asserted to be of minimum length among all paths satisfying \emph{all} of the stated conditions (start in $A$, end in $J \cup B$, $C$-$\sigma$-open); ``shortest'' is \emph{not} taken among all paths in $G$ from $A$ to $J \cup B$ irrespective of openness.
                    \end{itemize}

                \item There exist an integer $n \ge 0$ and a walk
                    \[
                        \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, v_{n-1}, a_{n-1}, v_n)
                    \]
                    in $G$ in the sense of def \ref{def:walks}, item~i., such that all four of the following conjuncts hold simultaneously:
                    \begin{itemize}
                        \item $v_0 \in A$;
                        \item $v_n \in J \cup B$;
                        \item $\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open in $G$ in the sense of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1., \emph{and} every collider of $\pi$ lies in $C$ (not merely in $\Anc^G(C)$):
                            \[
                                \forall\, k \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\},\; \bigl(k \text{ is a collider on } \pi \text{ in the sense of def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, item~ii.}\bigr) \implies v_k \in C.
                            \]
                            The trailing collider clause is a \emph{substantive strengthening} of $C$-$\sigma$-openness (whose collider clause, def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.i., only demands $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$), \emph{not} a translation of the walk-version of (2a); the strengthening is the LN's parenthetical ``and not just in $\Anc^G(C)$'' from Proposition \ref{prp:sigma_opens}(3). The non-collider clause of $C$-$\sigma$-openness (def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.ii.) is unchanged on $\pi$.
                        \item the length $n$ of $\pi$ is \emph{minimum} among all non-negative integers $m \ge 0$ for which there exists a walk $\pi' = (v'_0, a'_0, v'_1, \dots, v'_{m-1}, a'_{m-1}, v'_m)$ of length $m$ in $G$ (def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.) such that $v'_0 \in A$, $v'_m \in J \cup B$, $\pi'$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open in $G$ (def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.), \emph{and} every collider position $k' \in \{0, 1, \dots, m\}$ on $\pi'$ (in the sense of def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, item~ii.) has $v'_{k'} \in C$ --- that is, the minimization range is exactly the \emph{joint} class
                            \[
                                \mathcal{W}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open},\ \text{colliders} \subseteq C}
                                := \bigl\{\, \pi' \text{ walk in } G \,\bigm|\, \text{start-node of } \pi' \in A,\ \text{end-node of } \pi' \in J \cup B,\ \pi' \text{ is } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open},\ \forall\, k' \text{ collider of } \pi',\ v'_{k'} \in C \,\bigr\}.
                            \]
                            Per the addition tag \texttt{[shortest\_qualifier\_reference\_class\_ambiguous]}, this is the disambiguating reading for item~(b): the minimum is taken within the joint class ``$C$-$\sigma$-open walk \emph{and} all colliders in $C$'', \emph{not} within the wider class of $C$-$\sigma$-open walks alone (under which the witness would not necessarily have all colliders in $C$).
                    \end{itemize}
            \end{enumerate}
    \end{enumerate}

    \emph{Practice remark (preserved verbatim in content from the LN block).}
    In practice we usually check if every path from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked or not, i.e., we work with characterisation (1b) above. This is because, in contrast to walks, there are only a finite number of paths in a (finite) graph (def \ref{def:walks}, item~v.\ forbids repeated nodes, bounding the path length by $|J \cup V|$). In proofs, though, it often is easier to make use of walks --- i.e., characterisation (1a) and existential form (2b) --- since walks can be concatenated into walks (while one cannot in general concatenate two paths and again obtain a path).
\end{Rem}

% --- proof ------------------------------------------------------------------
\begin{proof}
The Remark is a list of four corollaries of Proposition \ref{prp:sigma_opens} (= \refrow{claim_3_23}, the three-way equivalence between (1) existence of a $C$-$\sigma$-open path between two nodes, (2) existence of a $C$-$\sigma$-open walk between them, and (3) existence of a $C$-$\sigma$-open walk between them whose colliders all lie in $C$). The four sub-items (1a), (1b), (2a), (2b) of the canonical statement are established separately below, each by a short argument that reduces, after a definitional unfolding of $\isPerp_G$ / $\nisPerp_G$ via def \ref{def:sigma_separation}, items~1--2, to a single direction of \ref{prp:sigma_opens} applied at the endpoints of a witnessing walk. The two ``shortest'' existence claims (2a), (2b) are routed through the well-ordering of $\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}$ once non-emptiness of the relevant minimisation class is established.

\medskip\noindent\textbf{(1a) $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ is equivalent to: every walk from $A$ to $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked.}
This is a direct definitional unfolding. By def \ref{def:sigma_separation}, item~1, the predicate $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ is defined as
\[
    \forall\, n \ge 0,\;\forall\, \text{walks } \pi = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, a_{n-1}, v_n) \text{ in } G \text{ (def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.) with } v_0 \in A,\, v_n \in J \cup B :\;
    \pi \text{ is } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-blocked},
\]
where ``$C$-$\sigma$-blocked'' is in the sense of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking} (equivalently, by the De Morgan paragraph of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, ``not $C$-$\sigma$-open'' per def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.). This is exactly the universal statement (1a). Hence (1a) and $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ are the same proposition. \qedsymbol\ (1a)

\medskip\noindent\textbf{(1b) $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ is equivalent to: every \emph{path} from $A$ to $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked.}
We prove the biconditional in two directions, using the equivalence (1a) established above to read $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ as the universal-over-walks statement.

\smallskip\noindent\emph{($\Rightarrow$) Direction.}
Assume $A \isPerp_G B \given C$, i.e.\ by (1a), every walk from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked. Let $\pi$ be a path from a node $v_0 \in A$ to a node $v_n \in J \cup B$ in the sense of def \ref{def:walks}, item~v. By def \ref{def:walks}, item~v., a path is in particular a walk in the sense of def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.\ (item~v.\ defines ``path'' as a walk satisfying the additional no-repeat constraint $\forall\, k, l \in \{0, 1, \dots, n\},\; k \ne l \implies v_k \ne v_l$; dropping the no-repeat constraint gives back the underlying walk-data). The universal-over-walks hypothesis therefore applies to $\pi$, and $\pi$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked. Since $\pi$ was an arbitrary path between $A$ and $J \cup B$, the universal-over-paths statement (1b) holds.

\smallskip\noindent\emph{($\Leftarrow$) Direction.}
Assume the universal-over-paths statement (1b): every path from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked. We show $A \isPerp_G B \given C$, equivalently (by (1a)) that every walk from $A$ to $J \cup B$ is $C$-$\sigma$-blocked. Suppose, for contradiction, that this fails: by the De Morgan dual of the universal over walks (def \ref{def:sigma_separation}, item~2, which spells out the negation of item~1 as an existential), there exist an integer $n \ge 0$ and a walk
\[
    \pi_w = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, a_{n-1}, v_n)
\]
in $G$ (def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.) with $v_0 \in A$, $v_n \in J \cup B$, and $\pi_w$ \emph{not} $C$-$\sigma$-blocked, i.e.\ $C$-$\sigma$-open in the sense of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.

The endpoint memberships $v_0 \in J \cup V$ and $v_n \in J \cup V$ required by \ref{prp:sigma_opens} follow from $v_0 \in A \subseteq J \cup V$ and $v_n \in J \cup B \subseteq J \cup V$ (the subset hypotheses $A, B \ins J \cup V$ are part of the standing setup of this Remark). Apply \ref{prp:sigma_opens} (= \refrow{claim_3_23}) at $w_1 := v_0$, $w_2 := v_n$: the existence of the $C$-$\sigma$-open walk $\pi_w$ between $v_0$ and $v_n$ is statement~(2) of \ref{prp:sigma_opens} at $(w_1, w_2) = (v_0, v_n)$, so the implication $(2) \Rightarrow (1)$ of \ref{prp:sigma_opens} yields a walk
\[
    \pi_p = (v'_0, a'_0, v'_1, a'_1, \dots, a'_{n' - 1}, v'_{n'})
\]
in $G$ with $v'_0 = v_0 \in A$ and $v'_{n'} = v_n \in J \cup B$, satisfying ($\pi_p$ is a path in $G$ in the sense of def \ref{def:walks}, item~v.) AND ($\pi_p$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open in the sense of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.). In particular, $\pi_p$ is a path from a node in $A$ to a node in $J \cup B$ that is \emph{not} $C$-$\sigma$-blocked (the De Morgan dual of $C$-$\sigma$-open per def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}). This contradicts the universal-over-paths hypothesis (1b). The assumption that some walk fails the blocking condition must therefore have been false, so $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ holds. \qedsymbol\ (1b)

\medskip\noindent\textbf{(2a) Under $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$, there exists a shortest $C$-$\sigma$-open path from $A$ to $J \cup B$.}
We must show: under the hypothesis $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$, the class
\[
    \mathcal{P}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}}
    := \bigl\{\, \pi' \text{ walk in } G \,\bigm|\, \text{start-node of } \pi' \in A,\ \text{end-node of } \pi' \in J \cup B,\ \pi' \text{ is a path},\ \pi' \text{ is } C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open} \,\bigr\}
\]
is non-empty, and that its set of lengths therefore admits a minimum (via the well-ordering of $\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}$). The minimum-length witness is the shortest $C$-$\sigma$-open path asserted by (2a).

\smallskip\noindent\emph{Non-emptiness of $\mathcal{P}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}}$.}
By def \ref{def:sigma_separation}, item~2, the negation $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$ is defined as: there exist an integer $n \ge 0$ and a walk
\[
    \pi_w = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, a_{n-1}, v_n)
\]
in $G$ (def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.) with $v_0 \in A$, $v_n \in J \cup B$, and $\pi_w$ \emph{not} $C$-$\sigma$-blocked, equivalently $C$-$\sigma$-open in the sense of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1. The endpoints satisfy $v_0 \in A \subseteq J \cup V$ and $v_n \in J \cup B \subseteq J \cup V$.

Apply \ref{prp:sigma_opens} (= \refrow{claim_3_23}) at $w_1 := v_0$, $w_2 := v_n$: the existence of the $C$-$\sigma$-open walk $\pi_w$ between $v_0$ and $v_n$ is statement~(2) at this pair of endpoints, so the implication $(2) \Rightarrow (1)$ of \ref{prp:sigma_opens} yields a walk $\pi_p$ in $G$ from $v_0$ to $v_n$ that is simultaneously a path (def \ref{def:walks}, item~v.) and $C$-$\sigma$-open (def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.). All four membership conditions of $\mathcal{P}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}}$ are met by $\pi_p$:
\begin{itemize}
    \item start-node $v_0 \in A$ (from $\pi_w$);
    \item end-node $v_n \in J \cup B$ (from $\pi_w$);
    \item $\pi_p$ is a path (output of \ref{prp:sigma_opens}, (2) $\Rightarrow$ (1) conjunct);
    \item $\pi_p$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open (output of \ref{prp:sigma_opens}, (2) $\Rightarrow$ (1) conjunct).
\end{itemize}
Hence $\pi_p \in \mathcal{P}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}}$, so the class is non-empty.

\smallskip\noindent\emph{Shortest witness via well-ordering of $\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}$.}
Consider the set of lengths
\[
    \mathcal{L} := \bigl\{\, \mathrm{len}(\pi') \;\bigm|\; \pi' \in \mathcal{P}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}} \,\bigr\}
    \;\subseteq\; \mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0},
\]
where the length $\mathrm{len}(\pi')$ of a walk $\pi'$ is the integer $n' \ge 0$ recording the number of edge positions in $\pi'$'s tuple (per the ``Length of a walk/path'' paragraph of the statement above, matching def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.). By the previous paragraph, $\mathcal{L} \ne \emptyset$ (it contains $\mathrm{len}(\pi_p)$). Since $\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}$ is well-ordered, the non-empty subset $\mathcal{L}$ has a minimum element $n^\star := \min \mathcal{L} \in \mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}$. Pick any $\pi^\star \in \mathcal{P}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}}$ with $\mathrm{len}(\pi^\star) = n^\star$ (such a $\pi^\star$ exists by the definition of $\mathcal{L}$). Then $\pi^\star$ satisfies all four conjuncts of (2a):
\begin{itemize}
    \item $v^\star_0 \in A$;
    \item $v^\star_{n^\star} \in J \cup B$;
    \item $\pi^\star$ is a path and $C$-$\sigma$-open (by membership in $\mathcal{P}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}}$);
    \item the length $n^\star$ of $\pi^\star$ is minimum among all $m \ge 0$ for which there exists a walk $\pi' \in \mathcal{P}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}}$ of length $m$ (by definition of $n^\star = \min \mathcal{L}$).
\end{itemize}
As emphasised by the addition tag \texttt{[shortest\_qualifier\_reference\_class\_ambiguous]} recorded in the statement above, the minimisation is taken in the \emph{joint} class $\mathcal{P}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}}$ (paths that are simultaneously $C$-$\sigma$-open and have endpoints in the prescribed sets), not in the wider class of all paths in $G$ from $A$ to $J \cup B$ irrespective of $\sigma$-openness; the existential (2a) is thereby established. \qedsymbol\ (2a)

\medskip\noindent\textbf{(2b) Under $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$, there exists a shortest $C$-$\sigma$-open walk from $A$ to $J \cup B$ with all colliders in $C$.}
The argument is parallel to (2a), but routed through the implication $(2) \Rightarrow (3)$ of \ref{prp:sigma_opens} (instead of $(2) \Rightarrow (1)$). The minimisation class is the joint class of walks satisfying \emph{both} $C$-$\sigma$-openness and the collider-strengthening clause; the strengthening is the substantive content of (3) of \ref{prp:sigma_opens} relative to (2), and not a translation of the path-version (2a) into walk-language.

\smallskip\noindent\emph{Non-emptiness of $\mathcal{W}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open},\,\text{colliders} \subseteq C}$.}
By def \ref{def:sigma_separation}, item~2, the hypothesis $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$ yields an integer $n \ge 0$ and a walk
\[
    \pi_w = (v_0, a_0, v_1, a_1, \dots, a_{n-1}, v_n)
\]
in $G$ (def \ref{def:walks}, item~i.) with $v_0 \in A$, $v_n \in J \cup B$, and $\pi_w$ $C$-$\sigma$-open in the sense of def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1. The endpoints satisfy $v_0 \in A \subseteq J \cup V$ and $v_n \in J \cup B \subseteq J \cup V$.

Apply \ref{prp:sigma_opens} (= \refrow{claim_3_23}) at $w_1 := v_0$, $w_2 := v_n$: the $C$-$\sigma$-open walk $\pi_w$ between $v_0$ and $v_n$ is statement~(2) of \ref{prp:sigma_opens} at this pair of endpoints, so the implication $(2) \Rightarrow (3)$ yields a walk $\pi_3$ in $G$ from $v_0$ to $v_n$ that is simultaneously $C$-$\sigma$-open (def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.) AND has every collider position $k \in \{0, 1, \dots, m\}$ on $\pi_3$ (in the sense of def \ref{def:collider_noncollider}, item~ii.) satisfying $v_{3, k} \in C$ -- this is the second conjunct of (3) of \ref{prp:sigma_opens}, which strengthens the $C$-$\sigma$-open collider clause $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$ to $v_k \in C$ (cf.\ the LN parenthetical ``and not just in $\Anc^G(C)$''). All four membership conditions of
\[
    \mathcal{W}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open},\,\text{colliders} \subseteq C}
\]
are met by $\pi_3$:
\begin{itemize}
    \item start-node $v_0 \in A$ (from $\pi_w$);
    \item end-node $v_n \in J \cup B$ (from $\pi_w$);
    \item $\pi_3$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open (first conjunct of (3) $\Rightarrow$ (3)'s output);
    \item every collider position on $\pi_3$ has node-label in $C$ (second conjunct).
\end{itemize}
Hence $\pi_3 \in \mathcal{W}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open},\,\text{colliders} \subseteq C}$, so the class is non-empty.

\smallskip\noindent\emph{Shortest witness via well-ordering of $\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}$.}
Consider the set of lengths
\[
    \mathcal{L}' := \bigl\{\, \mathrm{len}(\pi') \;\bigm|\; \pi' \in \mathcal{W}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open},\,\text{colliders} \subseteq C} \,\bigr\}
    \;\subseteq\; \mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}.
\]
By the previous paragraph, $\mathcal{L}' \ne \emptyset$ (it contains $\mathrm{len}(\pi_3)$). Since $\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}$ is well-ordered, $\mathcal{L}'$ has a minimum element $n^\star := \min \mathcal{L}' \in \mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}$. Pick any $\pi^\star \in \mathcal{W}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open},\,\text{colliders} \subseteq C}$ with $\mathrm{len}(\pi^\star) = n^\star$. Then $\pi^\star$ satisfies all four conjuncts of (2b):
\begin{itemize}
    \item $v^\star_0 \in A$;
    \item $v^\star_{n^\star} \in J \cup B$;
    \item $\pi^\star$ is $C$-$\sigma$-open and every collider position on $\pi^\star$ has node-label in $C$ (by membership in the joint class);
    \item the length $n^\star$ of $\pi^\star$ is minimum among all $m \ge 0$ for which there exists a walk $\pi' \in \mathcal{W}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open},\,\text{colliders} \subseteq C}$ of length $m$ (by definition of $n^\star = \min \mathcal{L}'$).
\end{itemize}
Two points of emphasis (per the wording-check addition tag \texttt{[shortest\_qualifier\_reference\_class\_ambiguous]} and the wording-check subtlety \texttt{walk\_colliders\_strictly\_in\_C\_is\_strengthening\_not\_translation} recorded in the statement above):
\begin{itemize}
    \item The minimisation is taken in the \emph{joint} class $\mathcal{W}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open},\,\text{colliders} \subseteq C}$ -- walks that are $C$-$\sigma$-open \emph{and} have all colliders in $C$ -- \emph{not} in the wider class of $C$-$\sigma$-open walks alone (in which case the witness would not necessarily have all colliders in $C$).
    \item The all-colliders-in-$C$ clause is a \emph{strict strengthening} of $C$-$\sigma$-openness: the $C$-$\sigma$-open collider clause (def \ref{def:sigma_blocking}, item~1.i.) only forces $v_k \in \Anc^G(C)$, whereas the (2b) clause forces $v_k \in C$ itself. This is precisely the LN's parenthetical ``and not just in $\Anc^G(C)$'' from statement~(3) of \ref{prp:sigma_opens}; the existential (2b) inherits it verbatim by the application of \ref{prp:sigma_opens}, $(2) \Rightarrow (3)$ above.
\end{itemize}
Hence the existential (2b) is established. \qedsymbol\ (2b)

\medskip\noindent\textbf{Conclusion.}
The four sub-statements (1a), (1b), (2a), (2b) of the canonical statement above have each been established separately:
\begin{itemize}
    \item (1a) is the definitional unfolding of $A \isPerp_G B \given C$ via def \ref{def:sigma_separation}, item~1.
    \item (1b) is the path-version equivalent of (1a), with the substantive direction $(\Leftarrow)$ routed through \ref{prp:sigma_opens}, $(2) \Rightarrow (1)$.
    \item (2a) is the existence-of-shortest-$C$-$\sigma$-open-path consequence of $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$, routed through \ref{prp:sigma_opens}, $(2) \Rightarrow (1)$ (for non-emptiness of $\mathcal{P}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open}}$) and well-ordering of $\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}$ (for the minimum-length witness).
    \item (2b) is the existence-of-shortest-$C$-$\sigma$-open-walk-with-colliders-in-$C$ consequence of $A \nisPerp_G B \given C$, routed through \ref{prp:sigma_opens}, $(2) \Rightarrow (3)$ (for non-emptiness of $\mathcal{W}_{A \to J \cup B}^{C\text{-}\sigma\text{-open},\,\text{colliders} \subseteq C}$) and well-ordering of $\mathbb{Z}_{\ge 0}$ (for the minimum-length witness).
\end{itemize}
This establishes the full Remark.
\end{proof}
